title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Browser and Node Client Systems · Narrowing Workshop
summary: An accessibility product team integrating a staging browser API must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for http caching, freshness, validators, vary, and invalidation, producing spec-aware cache decision notebook. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch spec-aware cache decision notebook while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Private Shared Cache
steps.instruction:
workshop case — accessible staging dashboard SDK stores a response with max-age=50, ETag "rev-5", Age:5, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Private Shared Cache.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts. For Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Private Shared Cache, keep Private Shared Cache connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
